headlines.title: Is Diamondback Energy (FANG) Above Fair Value After Recent Gains?
headlines.summary: After a strong multi year run, Diamondback Energy now looks closer to fairly priced, which raises the question of whether the recent share gains still leave enough valuation upside for new money. Diamondback Energy has returned 175.2% over the past 5 years, a pace that means recent buyers may be paying a much fuller price than earlier investors did.

headlines.title: Is Diamondback Energy (FANG) Fairly Valued On Its New Defensive Index Status?
headlines.summary: Diamondback Energy (FANG) has just been added to both the Russell 1000 Defensive Index and the Russell 1000 Value-Defensive Index, a classification shift that may influence how institutional investors view the stock. See our latest analysis for Diamondback Energy. headlines.title: Diamondback Energy (FANG) Stock Slides as Market Rises: Facts to Know Before You Trade
headlines.summary: In the latest trading session, Diamondback Energy (FANG) closed at $182, marking a -2.47% move from the previous day.

headlines.title: Diamondback Energy (FANG) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
headlines.summary: Shares of oil and gas producer Diamondback Energy (NASDAQ:FANG) fell 2.5% in the afternoon session after crude oil prices pulled back from the previous day's rally.

headlines.title: The S&P 500 Is Up 5% This Year. This ‘Cash Cow’ Fund Is Up 9%
headlines.summary: Investors holding Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:VOO) own a slice of the cheapest, largest cap-weighted index fund on the market, and it has been doing its job. VOO is up 10.25% year-to-date through July 7, 2026, riding a benchmark that continues to be dominated by mega-cap technology. The pitch to swap VOO for the Pacer ... The S&P 500 Is Up 5% This Year.

description: Diamondback Energy, Inc., an independent oil and natural gas company, acquires, develops, explores, and exploits unconventional, onshore oil and natural gas reserves in the Permian Basin in West Texas, the United States. The company primarily focuses on the development of the Spraberry and Wolfcamp formations of the Midland Basin; and the Wolfcamp and Bone Spring formations of the Delaware Basin, both of which are part of the Permian Basin in West Texas and New Mexico. Diamondback Energy, Inc. was founded in 2007 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.
